revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Softening of Evil Hearts]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Softening of Evil Hearts|Softening of Evil Hearts]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}
{{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} <!--removing this template because now we do not need it. All everybodywiki edit histories have been imported-->

<!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Content from {{ill|Folk_Orthodoxy|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk_Orthodoxy|qq=}}<!--{{s11}}---><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts-->

According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>

[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Softening of Evil Hearts]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Softening of Evil Hearts|Softening of Evil Hearts]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}
{{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} <!--removing this template because now we do not need it. All everybodywiki edit histories have been imported-->

E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Softening of Evil Hearts]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Softening of Evil Hearts|Softening of Evil Hearts]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

<!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Content from {{ill|Folk_Orthodoxy|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk_Orthodoxy|qq=}}<!--{{s11}}---><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts--><!--Softening of Evil Hearts-->

According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}

* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Softening of Evil Hearts]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Softening of Evil Hearts|Softening of Evil Hearts]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

revision.comment: 90 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ru:Умягчение злых сердец up to rev 138172264
revision.text:
{{Икона |название = Икона Богородицы
«Умягчение злых сердец» |оригинал = |изображение = Umyagchenie zlukh serdets 19th century icon.jpg |ширина = 300px |подпись = Богоматерь «Умягчения злых сердец» (фрагмент иконы «Нечаянная радость со 120 чудотворными иконами Богоматери», первая половина XIX века) |дата появления = |иконографический тип = |место нахождения = |чтимые списки = |дата празднования = в [[Неделя всех святых|Неделю всех святых]] |Викисклад = Category:Umyagchenie zlukh serdets }} : ''Существуют иконы со сходным сюжетом: [[Семистрельная икона Божией Матери]] и [[Страстная икона Божией Матери#Страстная икона «И тебе самой душу пройдёт оружие»|Страстная икона «И тебе самой душу пройдёт оружие»]]'' '''Умягчение злых сердец''', или ''Симеоново проречение'' — [[икона]] [[Богородица|Богородицы]], на которой символическими знаками изображено пророчество святого [[Симеон Богоприимец|Симеона Богоприимца]], произнесённое им в [[Иерусалимский храм|Иерусалимском храме]] в день [[Сретение Господне|Сретения Господня]]: {{НЗ|И благословил их Симеон и сказал Марии, Матери Его: ''се, лежит Сей на падение и на восстание многих в Израиле и в предмет пререканий, — и Тебе Самой оружие пройдет душу, — да откроются помышления многих сердец.|Лк|2:34|-35}} Образ «Умягчение злых сердец» происходит, по всей видимости, из Юго-Западной Руси, однако исторических сведений о нём, не имеется{{Cite web |url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html |title=Надежда Дмитриева. Икона Божией Матери «Умягчение злых сердец». |access-date=2022-08-30 |archive-date=2022-08-30 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html |deadlink=no }}. На иконе Богоматерь изображается с семью воткнутыми в [[сердце]] [[меч]]ами: по три справа и слева и один внизу. Число семь обозначает полноту горя, печали и сердечной боли, испытанных Богородицей в её земной жизни[https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html Икона Божией Матери «Умягчение злых сердец»]. {{Wayback|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html |date=20220830194506 }}. Православный календарь.. Иногда на коленях Богородицы изображается и Предвечный Младенец. Похожее изображение имеет также [[Семистрельная икона Божией Матери]]. Разница только в том, что мечи на ней расположены несколько иначе — три с одной и четыре с другой стороны. Празднование иконе совершается в [[Неделя всех святых|неделю Всех Святых]] (первое воскресенье после [[День Святой Троицы|Пятидесятницы]]). == Гимнография == [[Молитва]] перед иконой Божией Матери «Умягчение злых сердец»: {{начало цитаты}} О Многострадальная Мати Божия, Превысшая всех дщерей земли по чистоте Своей и по множеству страданий, Тобою на земли перенесенных! Приими многоболезненныя воздыхания наша и сохрани нас под кровом Твоея милости. Инаго бо прибежища и теплаго предстательства, разве Тебе, не вемы, но яко дерзновение имущи ко иже из Тебе Рожденному, помози и спаси ны молитвами Своими, да непреткновенно достигнем Царствия Небеснаго, идеже со всеми святыми будем воспевати в Троице Единаго Бога, всегда, ныне и присно и во веки веков. Аминь.{{конец цитаты}} Пред иконой молятся об исцелении от холеры, хромоты и расслабления, об умиротворении враждующих и об умягчении злых сердец, при вражде или гонениях. == Примечания == {{примечания}} == Литература == * {{книга |автор = [[Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|Тальберг Н. Д.]] |заглавие = Пространный месяцеслов Святых в Земле Российской просиявших. В каких нуждах каким иконам надлежит поклонятся |издание = Репринтное воспроизведение издания |место = СПб. |издательство = Вера |год = 1997 |страницы = 578 }} [[Категория:Православная иконография Богородицы|У]] [[Категория:Иконы Богоматери по алфавиту]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Умягчение злых сердец]] to [[Softening of Evil Hearts]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Икона |название = Икона Богородицы
«Умягчение злых сердец» |оригинал = |изображение = Umyagchenie zlukh serdets 19th century icon.jpg |ширина = 300px |подпись = Богоматерь «Умягчения злых сердец» (фрагмент иконы «Нечаянная радость со 120 чудотворными иконами Богоматери», первая половина XIX века) |дата появления = |иконографический тип = |место нахождения = |чтимые списки = |дата празднования = в [[Неделя всех святых|Неделю всех святых]] |Викисклад = Category:Umyagchenie zlukh serdets }} : ''Существуют иконы со сходным сюжетом: [[Семистрельная икона Божией Матери]] и [[Страстная икона Божией Матери#Страстная икона «И тебе самой душу пройдёт оружие»|Страстная икона «И тебе самой душу пройдёт оружие»]]'' '''Умягчение злых сердец''', или ''Симеоново проречение'' — [[икона]] [[Богородица|Богородицы]], на которой символическими знаками изображено пророчество святого [[Симеон Богоприимец|Симеона Богоприимца]], произнесённое им в [[Иерусалимский храм|Иерусалимском храме]] в день [[Сретение Господне|Сретения Господня]]: {{НЗ|И благословил их Симеон и сказал Марии, Матери Его: ''се, лежит Сей на падение и на восстание многих в Израиле и в предмет пререканий, — и Тебе Самой оружие пройдет душу, — да откроются помышления многих сердец.|Лк|2:34|-35}} Образ «Умягчение злых сердец» происходит, по всей видимости, из Юго-Западной Руси, однако исторических сведений о нём, не имеется{{Cite web |url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html |title=Надежда Дмитриева. Икона Божией Матери «Умягчение злых сердец». |access-date=2022-08-30 |archive-date=2022-08-30 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html |deadlink=no }}. На иконе Богоматерь изображается с семью воткнутыми в [[сердце]] [[меч]]ами: по три справа и слева и один внизу. Число семь обозначает полноту горя, печали и сердечной боли, испытанных Богородицей в её земной жизни[https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html Икона Божией Матери «Умягчение злых сердец»]. {{Wayback|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html |date=20220830194506 }}. Православный календарь.. Иногда на коленях Богородицы изображается и Предвечный Младенец. Похожее изображение имеет также [[Семистрельная икона Божией Матери]]. Разница только в том, что мечи на ней расположены несколько иначе — три с одной и четыре с другой стороны. Празднование иконе совершается в [[Неделя всех святых|неделю Всех Святых]] (первое воскресенье после [[День Святой Троицы|Пятидесятницы]]). == Гимнография == [[Молитва]] перед иконой Божией Матери «Умягчение злых сердец»: {{начало цитаты}} О Многострадальная Мати Божия, Превысшая всех дщерей земли по чистоте Своей и по множеству страданий, Тобою на земли перенесенных! Приими многоболезненныя воздыхания наша и сохрани нас под кровом Твоея милости. Инаго бо прибежища и теплаго предстательства, разве Тебе, не вемы, но яко дерзновение имущи ко иже из Тебе Рожденному, помози и спаси ны молитвами Своими, да непреткновенно достигнем Царствия Небеснаго, идеже со всеми святыми будем воспевати в Троице Единаго Бога, всегда, ныне и присно и во веки веков. Аминь.{{конец цитаты}} Пред иконой молятся об исцелении от холеры, хромоты и расслабления, об умиротворении враждующих и об умягчении злых сердец, при вражде или гонениях. == Примечания == {{примечания}} == Литература == * {{книга |автор = [[Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|Тальберг Н. Д.]] |заглавие = Пространный месяцеслов Святых в Земле Российской просиявших. В каких нуждах каким иконам надлежит поклонятся |издание = Репринтное воспроизведение издания |место = СПб. |издательство = Вера |год = 1997 |страницы = 578 }} [[Категория:Православная иконография Богородицы|У]] [[Категория:Иконы Богоматери по алфавиту]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Softening of Evil Hearts]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Softening of Evil Hearts|Softening of Evil Hearts]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Softening of Evil Hearts]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Softening of Evil Hearts|Softening of Evil Hearts]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.--> {{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M. // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr> : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}: {{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}
The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.
== Iconography == The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.
The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
== Hymnography == A traditional prayer before the icon: {{начало цитаты}} O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen. {{конец цитаты}}
== Veneration == Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=578}}
==See Also==
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

[[File:Umyagchenie_zlukh_serdets.jpg|thumb]]{{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0" />.-->
{{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M.  // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes  / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr>  : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}:
{{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}

The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.

== Iconography ==
The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.

The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.

The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

== Hymnography ==
A traditional prayer before the icon:
{{начало цитаты}}
O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen.
{{конец цитаты}}

== Veneration ==
Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.

== References ==
{{примечания}}

O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen.
{{конец цитаты}}

== Veneration ==
Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.

{{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M.  // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes  / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr>  : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}:
{{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}

The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.

== Iconography ==
The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.

The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.

The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

== Hymnography ==
A traditional prayer before the icon:
{{начало цитаты}}
O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen.
{{конец цитаты}}

== Veneration ==
Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.

== References ==
{{примечания}}

{{ill|ru:Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Умягчение злых сердец|qq=}}In {{ill|Bailichka|lt=bylichka|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bailichka|qq=}}and spiritual verses, Paraskeva Friday complains that she is not honored by not observing the prohibition on Fridays – they prick her with spindles, spin her hair, clog her eyes {{ill|Shives|lt=Kostrakostra|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shives|qq=}}. According to beliefs, {{ill|Paraskeva Friday|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paraskeva Friday|qq=}}is depicted on icons with spokes or spindles sticking out of her chest (cf. images of {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|lt=Seven-Strength Icon|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords=Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} , {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}).<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M.  // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes  / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr>  : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Богородицы «Умягчение злых сердец») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, also known as ''Simeon’s Prophecy''. It depicts the prophecy of {{ill|Simeon the God-Receiver|ru|Симеон Богоприимец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simeon the God-Receiver|qq=}} spoken during the {{ill|Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|ru|Сретение Господне|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Presentation of Jesus at the Temple|qq=}}:
{{НЗ|“Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel… and a sword will pierce through your own soul also.”|Lk|2:34|-35}}

The image likely originated from southwestern Rus', though its early history is unknown<ref name="Dmitrieva">{{Cite web|url=https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|title=Nadezhda Dmitrieva. Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|access-date=2022-08-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830195319/https://pravoslavie.ru/1915.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>.

== Iconography ==
The Mother of God is depicted with seven swords piercing her heart—three on each side and one below—symbolizing the fullness of sorrow experienced during her life<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Umyagchenie Zlykh Serdets'|archive-url=https://www.pravmir.ru/pravoslavnyj-kalendar/icons1/ikona-umyagchenie.html|archive-date=2022-08-30}}</ref>. In some versions, the Christ Child is depicted on her lap<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.

The icon is closely related to the {{ill|Semistrelnaya Icon|ru|Семистрельная икона Божией Матери|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semistrelnaya Icon|qq=}}, differing only in the arrangement of the swords—three on one side and four on the other<ref name="Dmitrieva"/>.

The feast day is observed on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

== Hymnography ==
A traditional prayer before the icon:
{{начало цитаты}}
O Most Sorrowful Mother of God, higher than all daughters of the earth in purity and suffering, accept our sorrowful sighs and shelter us under Your mercy. You alone are our refuge and intercessor before Your Son. Help us, guide us, and lead us to the Heavenly Kingdom. Amen.
{{конец цитаты}}

== Veneration ==
Believers pray before the icon for peace, reconciliation, and deliverance from enmity and persecution. It is also invoked for healing from cholera, lameness, and paralysis.

== References ==
{{примечания}}

== Literature ==
